title: Achnatherum haussknechtii
intro: Achnatherum haussknechtii, also known as Haussknecht's oat grass, is a perennial plant species native to the mountainous regions of northwestern North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Achnatherum haussknechtii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusate, and is sometimes combined with other herbs to create teas and infusions for respiratory health.
taxonomy: Achnatherum haussknechtii is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Achnatherum and is a perennial grass native to the Americas. Achnatherum haussknechtii is also known as Haussknecht's needlegrass or simply needlegrass. This species is further classified within the subfamily Pooideae and is closely related to other needlegrasses. The classification of Achnatherum haussknechtii reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the grass family.
